verdict.answer_first: B: starts with the direct answer and flaw in the first line, 'No — it has a serious flaw', then immediately names 'plaintext password comparison' without extra setup.
verdict.decisiveness: B: gives one immediately usable path, 'Store a salted hash ... and verify against it' plus `await bcrypt.compare(...)`; A is also clear but adds a broader signup/login sketch and migration offer.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because the core replacement is a compact drop-in pattern: `const ok = await bcrypt.compare(req.body.password, user.passwordHash); if (ok) issueToken(user);`.
